summary:
The minimal effective dose linked to potential tooth staining due to tetracyclines exceeds approximately 20 mg per kg body weight; hence, it suggests avoiding doses near or slightly exceeding this limit when treating pediatric patients. Therefore,

$\boxed{\text{Option B}}$ fits as the most accurate answer based on available evidence.
